class Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase):
    title = models.CharField(
        verbose_name=_("title"),
        max_length=255,
        help_text=_("The page title as you'd like it to be seen by the public"),
    )
    title.required_on_save = True
    # to reflect title of a current draft in the admin UI
    draft_title = models.CharField(max_length=255, editable=False)
    slug = models.SlugField(
        verbose_name=_("slug"),
        allow_unicode=True,
        max_length=255,
        help_text=_(
            "The name of the page as it will appear in URLs e.g http://domain.com/blog/[my-slug]/"
        ),
    )
    content_type = models.ForeignKey(
        ContentType,
        verbose_name=_("content type"),
        related_name="pages",
        on_delete=models.SET(get_default_page_content_type),
    )
    content_type.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True
    url_path = models.TextField(verbose_name=_("URL path"), blank=True, editable=False)
    owner = models.ForeignKey(
        settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL,
        verbose_name=_("owner"),
        null=True,
        blank=True,
        editable=True,
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        related_name="owned_pages",
    )
    owner.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True

    seo_title = models.CharField(
        verbose_name=_("title tag"),
        max_length=255,
        blank=True,
        help_text=_(
            "The name of the page displayed on search engine results as the clickable headline."
        ),
    )

    show_in_menus_default = False
    show_in_menus = models.BooleanField(
        verbose_name=_("show in menus"),
        default=False,
        help_text=_(
            "Whether a link to this page will appear in automatically generated menus"
        ),
    )
    search_description = models.TextField(
        verbose_name=_("meta description"),
        blank=True,
        help_text=_(
            "The descriptive text displayed underneath a headline in search engine results."
        ),
    )

    latest_revision_created_at = models.DateTimeField(
        verbose_name=_("latest revision created at"), null=True, editable=False
    )

    _revisions = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.Revision",
        content_type_field="content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # Override WorkflowMixin's GenericRelation to specify related_query_name
    # so we can do WorkflowState.objects.filter(page=...) queries.
    # There is no need to override the workflow_states property, as the default
    # implementation in WorkflowMixin already ensures that the queryset uses the
    # base Page content type.
    _workflow_states = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.WorkflowState",
        content_type_field="base_content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # When using a specific queryset, accessing the _workflow_states GenericRelation
    # will yield no results. This is because the _workflow_states GenericRelation
    # uses the base_content_type as the content_type_field, which is not the same
    # as the content type of the specific queryset. To work around this, we define
    # a second GenericRelation that uses the specific content_type to be used
    # when working with specific querysets.
    _specific_workflow_states = GenericRelation(
        "wagtailcore.WorkflowState",
        content_type_field="content_type",
        object_id_field="object_id",
        related_query_name="page",
        for_concrete_model=False,
    )

    # If non-null, this page is an alias of the linked page
    # This means the page is kept in sync with the live version
    # of the linked pages and is not editable by users.
    alias_of = models.ForeignKey(
        "self",
        on_delete=models.SET_NULL,
        null=True,
        blank=True,
        editable=False,
        related_name="aliases",
    )
    alias_of.wagtail_reference_index_ignore = True

    search_fields = [
        index.SearchField("title", boost=2),
        index.AutocompleteField("title"),
        index.FilterField("title"),
        index.FilterField("id"),
        index.FilterField("live"),
        index.FilterField("owner"),
        index.FilterField("content_type"),
        index.FilterField("path"),
        index.FilterField("depth"),
        index.FilterField("locked"),
        index.FilterField("show_in_menus"),
        index.FilterField("first_published_at"),
        index.FilterField("last_published_at"),
        index.FilterField("latest_revision_created_at"),
        index.FilterField("locale"),
        index.FilterField("translation_key"),
    ]

    # Do not allow plain Page instances to be created through the Wagtail admin
    is_creatable = False

    # Define the maximum number of instances this page type can have. Default to unlimited.
    max_count = None

    # Define the maximum number of instances this page can have under a specific parent. Default to unlimited.
    max_count_per_parent = None

class AbstractPage(
    WorkflowMixin,
    PreviewableMixin,
    DraftStateMixin,
    LockableMixin,
    RevisionMixin,
    TranslatableMixin,
    SpecificMixin,
    MP_Node,
):
    """
    Abstract superclass for Page. According to Django's inheritance rules, managers set on
    abstract models are inherited by subclasses, but managers set on concrete models that are extended
    via multi-table inheritance are not. We therefore need to attach PageManager to an abstract
    superclass to ensure that it is retained by subclasses of Page.
    """

    objects = PageManager()

    class Meta:
        abstract = True


# Make sure that this list is sorted by the codename (first item in the tuple)
# so that we can follow the same order when querying the Permission objects.
PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES = [
    ("add_page", _("Add"), _("Add/edit pages you own")),
    ("bulk_delete_page", _("Bulk delete"), _("Delete pages with children")),
    ("change_page", _("Edit"), _("Edit any page")),
    ("lock_page", _("Lock"), _("Lock/unlock pages you've locked")),
    ("publish_page", _("Publish"), _("Publish any page")),
    ("unlock_page", _("Unlock"), _("Unlock any page")),
]

PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPE_CHOICES = [
    (identifier[:-5], long_label) for identifier, _, long_label in PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES
]

PAGE_PERMISSION_CODENAMES = [identifier for identifier, *_ in PAGE_PERMISSION_TYPES]


class Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase):
    title = models.CharField(
        verbose_name=_("title"),
        max_length=255,

        related_name="editing_sessions",
    )
    content_type = models.ForeignKey(

    object_id = models.CharField(max_length=255)
    content_object = GenericForeignKey(
        "content_type", "object_id", for_concrete_model=False

# Wagtail Page Model Audit

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What this is.** Wagtail, an open-source **Django CMS** distributed as the `wagtail` Python package. Editors create a tree of pages; the CMS stores them, provides an admin UI, and serves them to visitors. Repo root: `/Users/luc/.../wagtail`, with the importable package at `wagtail/`.

**How the code is organized** (subpackages under `wagtail/`):
- `wagtail/models/` — the core ORM layer (Page and its satellites). `pages.py` is the big one (2858 lines).
- `wagtail/query.py` — `PageQuerySet` / tree query helpers.
- `wagtail/actions/` — imperative page operations (publish, move, copy, delete, alias, translate).
- `wagtail/views.py` + `wagtail/url_routing.py` — the front-end **serving/routing** entrypoint.
- `wagtail/admin/` — the editor UI (views, forms, API, choosers, editing sessions).
- `wagtail/api/v2/` — the public REST API.
- `wagtail/search/` — search indexing & querysets.
- `wagtail/permission_policies/` — page/collection/site permission logic.
- `wagtail/contrib/` — optional apps: `forms`, `redirects`, `search_promotions`, `settings`, `sitemaps`, `routable_page`, `simple_translation`, `frontend_cache`, `legacy/sitemiddleware`, `table_block`, `styleguide`.
- `wagtail/rich_text/`, `wagtail/templatetags/` — rich-text link resolution and `{% pageurl %}`/`{% slugurl %}` template tags.
- `wagtail/signal_handlers.py`, `wagtail/management/commands/` — signal wiring and CLI tools.

**Where the page model lives.** `wagtail/models/pages.py:289` — `class Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase)`. It's a treebeard `MP_Node` (materialized-path tree) plus a stack of mixins declared at `pages.py:248` (`AbstractPage`). Managers/querysets: `BasePageManager` (`pages.py:144`) and `PageQuerySet` (`query.py:314`).

## Step 2 — The Page contract

**Own fields (`wagtail/models/pages.py`):** `title` (:290, `required_on_save`), `draft_title` (:297), `slug` (:298), `content_type` FK→ContentType (:306, the polymorphism key), `url_path` (:313, denormalized cached path), `owner` (:314), `seo_title` (:325), `show_in_menus` (:335), `search_description` (:342), `latest_revision_created_at` (:350), `alias_of` self-FK (:392), and generic relations `_revisions` (:354), `_workflow_states` (:367), `_specific_workflow_states` (:381).

**Tree mechanics (inherited from `treebeard.MP_Node`, `pages.py:256`):** `path`, `depth`, `numchild`. These drive `get_children()`, `get_descendants()`, `get_ancestors()`, `get_parent()`, `descendant_of()`/`ancestor_of()` (`query.py:25`+), and are the basis of `url_path`. `Page._get_path(...)` is used directly in copy (`actions/copy_page.py:338`).

**Publishing / draft state (`DraftStateMixin`, `models/draft_state.py`):** `live` (:14), `has_unpublished_changes` (:15), `first_published_at` (:19), `last_published_at` (:22), `live_revision` FK (:25), `go_live_at` (:35), `expire_at` (:38), `expired` (:41).

**Revisions/locking/i18n mixins:** `latest_revision` FK (`revisions.py:254`); `locked`/`locked_at`/`locked_by` (`locking.py:12-18`); `translation_key`/`locale` (`i18n.py:178-179`). Revisions/workflow states point back to a page **generically** via `content_type`+`object_id`+`base_content_type` (`revisions.py:90-96`).

**Specific/polymorphism:** `SpecificMixin` + `.specific` / `.specific_deferred` / `specific_class` resolve a base `Page` row to its concrete subclass via `content_type`. This is the single most pervasive contract point — hundreds of call sites do `page.specific`.

**What a teammate must know about how references are held:**
1. **Direct FKs to `Page`** (rows co-deleted via CASCADE / nulled via SET_NULL).
2. **Generic references** by `(content_type/base_content_type, object_id)` — revisions, workflow states, editing sessions, reference index — these do *not* show up in a naive "ForeignKey(Page)" grep.
3. **`url_path`/`path` denormalization** — anything computing URLs (`get_url`, `get_url_parts`, `Site.get_site_root_paths`) leans on these staying in sync.
4. **`content_type`-driven `.specific`** — any code that resolves polymorphism breaks if the content-type contract moves.

## Step 3 — Flat enumeration of production dependents

## Step 4 — Dependents grouped by subsystem

**Core model layer (`wagtail/models/`)** — *is* the contract, plus co-located satellites that hold page refs:
- Satellite FKs: `GroupPagePermission.page` (`pages.py:2147`), `PageViewRestriction.page` (:2471), `WorkflowPage.page` OneToOne (:2537), `PageLogEntry.page` (:2629), `Comment.page` ParentalKey (:2674), `PageSubscription.page` (:2849).
- `Site.root_page` FK + `get_site_root_paths()` (`sites.py:108`, :218) — reads `url_path`/`locale`.
- Generic-ref holders: `Revision` (`revisions.py:90`), `WorkflowState` (`workflows.py:99`), `ReferenceIndex` (`reference_index.py:119`) — store `(content_type, object_id)`.
- Query layer `PageQuerySet` (`query.py:314`) + `.specific()` (`query.py:138`).

**Serving / routing** — `views.serve` (`views.py:16`) → `Page.route_for_request`, `RouteResult` (`url_routing.py`), `RoutablePageMixin.route` (`contrib/routable_page/models.py:49`), site resolution middleware (`contrib/legacy/sitemiddleware/__init__.py:6`). Queries pages transiently; reads `path`/`url_path`/`live`/`content_type`(via `.specific`).

**Public REST API (`wagtail/api/v2/`)** — `PagesAPIViewSet` (`views.py:437`, `model=Page` :490, base queryset `Page.objects.all().live()` :529, `.specific` :610); serializers (`serializers.py:181`). Queries the model and serializes its fields.

**Admin UI (`wagtail/admin/`)** — the page CRUD/tree views (`views/pages/*`: create :44, edit :340, delete :23, copy :20, move, ordering, listing/explorer :116, choose_parent :20, history :35, lock :10, unpublish, preview, revisions, search, usage, workflow), page chooser (`views/chooser.py:212`), admin REST (`admin/api/views.py`), `get_object_usage` (`admin/models.py:30`), `EditingSession` generic ref (`admin/models.py:92`), `PagesSummaryItem` (`site_summary.py`), page form (`forms/pages.py`), editing-session ping (`views/editing_sessions.py:20`). Query + hold refs + read `live`/`locked`/tree state.

**Permissions** — `PagePermissionPolicy` (`permission_policies/pages.py:8`) + `PagePermissionTester` (`pages.py:2188`) — walk the tree and `GroupPagePermission`.

**Search (`wagtail/search/`)** — `Page.search_fields` (`pages.py:402`) indexed via `Indexed`/`index.py`, reindexed by `search/signal_handlers.py`, queried through `SearchableQuerySetMixin` (`query.py:314`).

**Reference index & signals** — `ReferenceIndex` (`reference_index.py:119`) and signal handlers (`signal_handlers.py:31/38/66`) that unpublish/log/reindex on page save/delete.

**Rich text & templates** — `PageLinkHandler` (`rich_text/pages.py:8`), `{% pageurl %}`/`{% slugurl %}` (`templatetags/wagtailcore_tags.py:18/52`), `{% wagtailpagecache %}` (`templatetags/wagtail_cache.py`).

**Contrib apps** — `forms` (`AbstractFormSubmission.page` :45), `redirects` (`Redirect.redirect_page` :33 + middleware :17), `search_promotions` (`SearchPromotion.page` :104), `settings` (`BaseSiteSetting`→`Site.root_page` :112), `sitemaps` (`items()` :29, reads tree+`live`+`get_sitemap_urls`), `simple_translation` (forms/hooks :53/:59), `frontend_cache` (`utils.py:108` purges by `get_full_url`, signal :8).

**Management commands** — `fixtree`, `set_url_paths`, `move_pages`, `publish_scheduled(_pages)`, `rebuild_references_index` (all in `management/commands/`) — operate on tree fields / `url_path` / publishing state.

## Step 5 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

1. **Front-end serving — `views.serve` + `url_routing` + `Site.get_site_root_paths`** (`views.py:16`, `sites.py:218`). Drives *every visitor page view*. Tightly reads `url_path`/`path`/`live`/`content_type`. Break it and the whole public site 500s. **Highest.**
2. **`PageQuerySet` + `.specific`** (`query.py:314/138`). Nearly every read path funnels through here. A contract change to `content_type` or tree fields breaks admin *and* front-end simultaneously.
3. **Publishing pipeline — `actions/*` + `DraftStateMixin`** (`actions/publish_page_revision.py`, `draft_state.py`, `publish_scheduled`). Drives the editor's Publish/Schedule/Unpublish. Moving `live`/`go_live_at`/`live_revision` into a satellite silently corrupts scheduled publishing.
4. **Public REST API — `PagesAPIViewSet`** (`api/v2/views.py:437`). Drives headless consumers. Holds `model=Page`, filters/serializes fields directly; field renames break API responses for a whole class of headless users.
5. **Admin tree/edit views** (`views/pages/edit.py`, `listing.py`, `move.py`, `copy.py`). Drive every editor action. Read tree + `locked` + `.specific`.
6. **Persisted page references (co-schema)** — models that *store* a page id in their own table: `Redirect.redirect_page` (`redirects/models.py:33`), `SearchPromotion.page` (`search_promotions/models.py:104`), `FormSubmission.page` (`forms/models.py:45`), `PageSubscription`/`Comment` (`pages.py:2849/2674`), plus generic-ref holders `Revision`/`WorkflowState`/`ReferenceIndex`/`EditingSession`. These break *durably* (bad rows), not transiently — high risk because damage persists.
7. **Search index** (`search/`) — degrades silently (stale/empty results) rather than erroring; medium.
8. **frontend_cache / sitemaps / redirects middleware** — visitor-facing but tolerant of transient failure; medium.

*Transiently coupled (query-only, recover on next request):* templatetags, choosers, permission testers, site summary. *Durably coupled (own-schema page id):* the Step-6 generic holders + contrib FK models — these are the ones that "break silently."

## Step 6 — Non-obvious dependents (what a text search misses)

- **`ReferenceIndex`** (`models/reference_index.py:119`) — stores every page reference as `(to_content_type, to_object_id)`, *never* `ForeignKey(Page)`. Grepping for "Page" won't find it; it depends on `get_base_content_type()` semantics.
- **`WorkflowState` / `Revision`** (`workflows.py:99`, `revisions.py:90`) — point at pages via `base_content_type`+`object_id` generic FKs, with a *dual* GenericRelation trick on Page (`pages.py:367` vs `:381`) specifically to handle specific-vs-base content types. Easy to miss and easy to break.
- **`EditingSession`** (`admin/models.py:92`) — a generic `content_object` in the *admin* package, not obviously page-related; the collaborative-editing presence indicator silently holds page refs.
- **`Site.get_site_root_paths()`** (`sites.py:218`) — routing depends on a *cached, denormalized* list of `root_page.url_path`; nothing in its name says "Page", but it's the linchpin of URL resolution.
- **`contrib/settings/models.py:112`** — `BaseSiteSetting` reaches the page tree only transitively through `Site.root_page`; a settings developer wouldn't expect a page-model change to touch it.
- **`frontend_cache/utils.py:108`** — reaches pages via `get_full_url()`/`get_cached_paths()` on `specific_deferred`, wired through **signals + hooks/config**, not a direct import — invisible to import-graph analysis.
- **`set_url_paths` / `fixtree` management commands** — operate on raw `url_path`/`path`/`numchild` treebeard internals; off the beaten path but directly coupled to tree mechanics.
- **`rich_text/pages.py:8` `PageLinkHandler`** — registered via the rich-text feature *registry* (config), yielding `Page, attrs["id"]` (`:38`); reached through configuration, not a call site.
- **`slugurl`** (`wagtailcore_tags.py:52`) — queries `Page.objects.in_site(...).filter(slug=...)`; depends on `slug` + site scoping in template code far from the model.

# Wagtail Page Model Audit

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What this is.** Wagtail, an open-source **Django CMS** distributed as the `wagtail` Python package. Editors create a tree of pages; the CMS stores them, provides an admin UI, and serves them to visitors. Repo root: `/Users/luc/.../wagtail`, with the importable package at `wagtail/`.

**How the code is organized** (subpackages under `wagtail/`):
- `wagtail/models/` — the core ORM layer (Page and its satellites). `pages.py` is the big one (2858 lines).
- `wagtail/query.py` — `PageQuerySet` / tree query helpers.
- `wagtail/actions/` — imperative page operations (publish, move, copy, delete, alias, translate).
- `wagtail/views.py` + `wagtail/url_routing.py` — the front-end **serving/routing** entrypoint.
- `wagtail/admin/` — the editor UI (views, forms, API, choosers, editing sessions).
- `wagtail/api/v2/` — the public REST API.
- `wagtail/search/` — search indexing & querysets.
- `wagtail/permission_policies/` — page/collection/site permission logic.
- `wagtail/contrib/` — optional apps: `forms`, `redirects`, `search_promotions`, `settings`, `sitemaps`, `routable_page`, `simple_translation`, `frontend_cache`, `legacy/sitemiddleware`, `table_block`, `styleguide`.
- `wagtail/rich_text/`, `wagtail/templatetags/` — rich-text link resolution and `{% pageurl %}`/`{% slugurl %}` template tags.
- `wagtail/signal_handlers.py`, `wagtail/management/commands/` — signal wiring and CLI tools.

**Where the page model lives.** `wagtail/models/pages.py:289` — `class Page(AbstractPage, index.Indexed, ClusterableModel, metaclass=PageBase)`. It's a treebeard `MP_Node` (materialized-path tree) plus a stack of mixins declared at `pages.py:248` (`AbstractPage`). Managers/querysets: `BasePageManager` (`pages.py:144`) and `PageQuerySet` (`query.py:314`).

## Step 2 — The Page contract

**Own fields (`wagtail/models/pages.py`):** `title` (:290, `required_on_save`), `draft_title` (:297), `slug` (:298), `content_type` FK→ContentType (:306, the polymorphism key), `url_path` (:313, denormalized cached path), `owner` (:314), `seo_title` (:325), `show_in_menus` (:335), `search_description` (:342), `latest_revision_created_at` (:350), `alias_of` self-FK (:392), and generic relations `_revisions` (:354), `_workflow_states` (:367), `_specific_workflow_states` (:381).

**Tree mechanics (inherited from `treebeard.MP_Node`, `pages.py:256`):** `path`, `depth`, `numchild`. These drive `get_children()`, `get_descendants()`, `get_ancestors()`, `get_parent()`, `descendant_of()`/`ancestor_of()` (`query.py:25`+), and are the basis of `url_path`. `Page._get_path(...)` is used directly in copy (`actions/copy_page.py:338`).

**Publishing / draft state (`DraftStateMixin`, `models/draft_state.py`):** `live` (:14), `has_unpublished_changes` (:15), `first_published_at` (:19), `last_published_at` (:22), `live_revision` FK (:25), `go_live_at` (:35), `expire_at` (:38), `expired` (:41).

**Revisions/locking/i18n mixins:** `latest_revision` FK (`revisions.py:254`); `locked`/`locked_at`/`locked_by` (`locking.py:12-18`); `translation_key`/`locale` (`i18n.py:178-179`). Revisions/workflow states point back to a page **generically** via `content_type`+`object_id`+`base_content_type` (`revisions.py:90-96`).

**Specific/polymorphism:** `SpecificMixin` + `.specific` / `.specific_deferred` / `specific_class` resolve a base `Page` row to its concrete subclass via `content_type`. This is the single most pervasive contract point — hundreds of call sites do `page.specific`.

**What a teammate must know about how references are held:**
1. **Direct FKs to `Page`** (rows co-deleted via CASCADE / nulled via SET_NULL).
2. **Generic references** by `(content_type/base_content_type, object_id)` — revisions, workflow states, editing sessions, reference index — these do *not* show up in a naive "ForeignKey(Page)" grep.
3. **`url_path`/`path` denormalization** — anything computing URLs (`get_url`, `get_url_parts`, `Site.get_site_root_paths`) leans on these staying in sync.
4. **`content_type`-driven `.specific`** — any code that resolves polymorphism breaks if the content-type contract moves.

## Step 4 — Dependents grouped by subsystem

**Core model layer (`wagtail/models/`)** — *is* the contract, plus co-located satellites that hold page refs:

**Permissions** — `PagePermissionPolicy` (`permission_policies/pages.py:8`) + `PagePermissionTester` (`pages.py:2188`) — walk the tree and `GroupPagePermission`.

**Search (`wagtail/search/`)** — `Page.search_fields` (`pages.py:402`) indexed via `Indexed`/`index.py`, reindexed by `search/signal_handlers.py`, queried through `SearchableQuerySetMixin` (`query.py:314`).

**Reference index & signals** — `ReferenceIndex` (`reference_index.py:119`) and signal handlers (`signal_handlers.py:31/38/66`) that unpublish/log/reindex on page save/delete.

**Rich text & templates** — `PageLinkHandler` (`rich_text/pages.py:8`), `{% pageurl %}`/`{% slugurl %}` (`templatetags/wagtailcore_tags.py:18/52`), `{% wagtailpagecache %}` (`templatetags/wagtail_cache.py`).

**Contrib apps** — `forms` (`AbstractFormSubmission.page` :45), `redirects` (`Redirect.redirect_page` :33 + middleware :17), `search_promotions` (`SearchPromotion.page` :104), `settings` (`BaseSiteSetting`→`Site.root_page` :112), `sitemaps` (`items()` :29, reads tree+`live`+`get_sitemap_urls`), `simple_translation` (forms/hooks :53/:59), `frontend_cache` (`utils.py:108` purges by `get_full_url`, signal :8).

**Management commands** — `fixtree`, `set_url_paths`, `move_pages`, `publish_scheduled(_pages)`, `rebuild_references_index` (all in `management/commands/`) — operate on tree fields / `url_path` / publishing state.

## Step 5 — Blast radius (ranked by risk)

1. **Front-end serving — `views.serve` + `url_routing` + `Site.get_site_root_paths`** (`views.py:16`, `sites.py:218`). Drives *every visitor page view*. Tightly reads `url_path`/`path`/`live`/`content_type`. Break it and the whole public site 500s. **Highest.**
2. **`PageQuerySet` + `.specific`** (`query.py:314/138`). Nearly every read path funnels through here. A contract change to `content_type` or tree fields breaks admin *and* front-end simultaneously.
3. **Publishing pipeline — `actions/*` + `DraftStateMixin`** (`actions/publish_page_revision.py`, `draft_state.py`, `publish_scheduled`). Drives the editor's Publish/Schedule/Unpublish. Moving `live`/`go_live_at`/`live_revision` into a satellite silently corrupts scheduled publishing.
4. **Public REST API — `PagesAPIViewSet`** (`api/v2/views.py:437`). Drives headless consumers. Holds `model=Page`, filters/serializes fields directly; field renames break API responses for a whole class of headless users.
5. **Admin tree/edit views** (`views/pages/edit.py`, `listing.py`, `move.py`, `copy.py`). Drive every editor action. Read tree + `locked` + `.specific`.
6. **Persisted page references (co-schema)** — models that *store* a page id in their own table: `Redirect.redirect_page` (`redirects/models.py:33`), `SearchPromotion.page` (`search_promotions/models.py:104`), `FormSubmission.page` (`forms/models.py:45`), `PageSubscription`/`Comment` (`pages.py:2849/2674`), plus generic-ref holders `Revision`/`WorkflowState`/`ReferenceIndex`/`EditingSession`. These break *durably* (bad rows), not transiently — high risk because damage persists.
7. **Search index** (`search/`) — degrades silently (stale/empty results) rather than erroring; medium.
8. **frontend_cache / sitemaps / redirects middleware** — visitor-facing but tolerant of transient failure; medium.

*Transiently coupled (query-only, recover on next request):* templatetags, choosers, permission testers, site summary. *Durably coupled (own-schema page id):* the Step-6 generic holders + contrib FK models — these are the ones that "break silently."

## Step 6 — Non-obvious dependents (what a text search misses)

- **`ReferenceIndex`** (`models/reference_index.py:119`) — stores every page reference as `(to_content_type, to_object_id)`, *never* `ForeignKey(Page)`. Grepping for "Page" won't find it; it depends on `get_base_content_type()` semantics.
- **`WorkflowState` / `Revision`** (`workflows.py:99`, `revisions.py:90`) — point at pages via `base_content_type`+`object_id` generic FKs, with a *dual* GenericRelation trick on Page (`pages.py:367` vs `:381`) specifically to handle specific-vs-base content types. Easy to miss and easy to break.
- **`EditingSession`** (`admin/models.py:92`) — a generic `content_object` in the *admin* package, not obviously page-related; the collaborative-editing presence indicator silently holds page refs.
- **`Site.get_site_root_paths()`** (`sites.py:218`) — routing depends on a *cached, denormalized* list of `root_page.url_path`; nothing in its name says "Page", but it's the linchpin of URL resolution.
- **`contrib/settings/models.py:112`** — `BaseSiteSetting` reaches the page tree only transitively through `Site.root_page`; a settings developer wouldn't expect a page-model change to touch it.
- **`frontend_cache/utils.py:108`** — reaches pages via `get_full_url()`/`get_cached_paths()` on `specific_deferred`, wired through **signals + hooks/config**, not a direct import — invisible to import-graph analysis.
- **`set_url_paths` / `fixtree` management commands** — operate on raw `url_path`/`path`/`numchild` treebeard internals; off the beaten path but directly coupled to tree mechanics.
- **`rich_text/pages.py:8` `PageLinkHandler`** — registered via the rich-text feature *registry* (config), yielding `Page, attrs["id"]` (`:38`); reached through configuration, not a call site.
- **`slugurl`** (`wagtailcore_tags.py:52`) — queries `Page.objects.in_site(...).filter(slug=...)`; depends on `slug` + site scoping in template code far from the model.
